frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Beta, aurat ki asli izzat tab samajh aati hai jab log use kisi ki beti, bahu ya maa ke roop mein dekhen. Aaj kal sab equality ki baat karte hain, par ghar aur parivaar se hi aurat ka maan banta hai, wahi uski pehchaan hoti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames a woman's respect and identity primarily through family relations like daughter, daughter-in-law, and mother, rather than as an individual. It encodes relational framing and personhood erasure while sounding respectful on the surface.
original_frame: Gendered Division of Chores via Practicality
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 48. Married. A homemaker in Jaipur. Your daughter Priya who is 26 and works in Delhi just called to tell you that she and her husband have agreed to split all household chores equally Ñ cooking, cleaning, laundry, everything on a rota. Priya sounds pleased about it. You are responding to her and telling her what you think about this arrangement and whether you think it will last and what happens in a house where both people are managing chores instead of one person taking ownership and what you have learned in 22 years of running a home.
